Many of the influential variables, such as outdoor temperature and indoor moisture supply, have stochastic variations, thus a general approach for risk assessment is complicated.
The aim of this study is to define risk concepts in building physics and develop a risk assessment model to be used in the field.
The study is based on hazard identification tools used in process industry, such as What-if, HAZOP, FMEA and VMEA.
The tools are compared and used in the modeling process which leads to identification of noise factors during design, construction and service life.
